mnemonics.title: Constructor Order Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: Bases First, Left to Right - In multiple inheritance, base constructors are called in the order they are listed (left to right in declaration), then the derived class constructor runs. Destructors run in reverse.

mnemonics.title: Diamond Problem Solution
mnemonics.content: Virtual is Vital for Victory - Use 'virtual' keyword to prevent the diamond problem. Without virtual = double trouble, with virtual = single solution.

mnemonics.title: Ambiguity Resolution
mnemonics.content: Scope it Out with :: - When in doubt which member to use, scope resolution operator (::) will help you choose.

acronyms.expansion: Virtual Base Pointer Register - Pointer added to derived class object to track virtual base class locations in memory
acronyms.usage: Used by compiler to manage virtual inheritance and resolve addresses of virtual base class members

acronyms.expansion: Virtual Base Pointer - A pointer stored in the derived class that points to the virtual base class subobject
acronyms.usage: Added to class structure when virtual inheritance is used to access virtual base class members correctly

visualTips.visualization: Draw an inverted diamond: Top is common base class (A), two intermediate classes (B and C) below it, derived class (D) at bottom. Shows how D gets two paths to A without virtual inheritance.
Show one shared A subobject in memory with both B and C pointing to it. Use boxes to represent objects in memory with pointers showing the relationship.
Timeline showing: Base1() → Base2() → Derived() → ~Derived() → ~Base2() → ~Base1() - arrows flowing in sequence to show order.

analogies.analogy: Like a child inheriting traits from both mother (cooking skill) and father (mathematical ability) - the child gets combined abilities from both parents.
Two family trees converging to one ancestor - without virtual inheritance, the child has two separate copies of that ancestor's genes. With virtual inheritance, only one shared copy.
Like a shared Netflix subscription between two siblings - only one actual subscription (base class) but both can access it.
Like asking 'Which John?' when there are multiple Johns - you must specify which one (e.g., John from Marketing vs John from Engineering).
